# cantus

> A Python framework for writing LLM agent harnesses on Google Colab. Students compose five protocol kinds (`Skill`, `Analyzer`, `Validator`, `Workflow`, `Memory`) over a Gemma 4 backbone. This file is a single-shot context for *external* LLMs (Claude, GPT, Gemini, NotebookLM) so they can produce framework-style code on request.

## Versioning

- Current release: `v0.1.3`. Install with `pip install git+https://github.com/schola-cantorum/cantus@v0.1.3` (use `cantus[runtime]` extras for the Gemma 4 loader).
- Notebooks: open `notebooks/task_template.ipynb` in Colab via `https://colab.research.google.com/github/schola-cantorum/cantus/blob/v0.1.3/notebooks/task_template.ipynb`. The companion `notebooks/admin_setup.ipynb` mirrors Gemma 4 weights to Drive once per installation.

## When to use this file

Paste this whole file into a fresh chat with an external LLM, then ask it to write a new `@skill` / `@analyzer` / `@validator` / `@workflow`, or a `Memory` subclass. The output should `ast.parse` cleanly, use the right decorator and signature shape, and follow the docstring convention below.

## Public API surface

```python
from cantus import (
    # Decorator entries (most common)
    skill, analyzer, validator, workflow, debug,
    # Function-pass entries (intermediate)
    register_skill, register_analyzer, register_validator, register_workflow,
    # Class-first base classes (canonical / advanced)
    Skill, Analyzer, Validator, Workflow, Memory,
    # Memory implementations (class-only)
    ShortTermMemory, BM25Memory, EmbeddingMemory,
    # Runtime
    Action, CallSkillAction, FinalAnswerAction,
    Observation, SkillObservation, ToolErrorObservation,
    ValidationErrorObservation, MaxIterationsObservation,
    EventStream, Agent, Inspector,
    # Validator return type
    Result,
    # Loader
    mount_drive_and_load,
)
```

Memory has **no** decorator and **no** function-pass entry. `from cantus import memory` and `from cantus import register_memory` both raise `ImportError` by design — memory needs state, so a class is required.

## Skill — a tool the LLM can call

```python
from cantus import skill

@skill
def search_book(title: str, lang: str = "zh-TW") -> str:
    """Search the library catalog.

    Args:
        title: book title keyword.
        lang: locale hint, default zh-TW.

    Returns:
        A string with one line per match.
    """
    ...
```

The signature becomes the Pydantic args schema. The first paragraph of the docstring becomes the LLM-facing description. The `Args:` block becomes per-arg descriptions.

Function-pass form: `register_skill(search_book)`.
Class-first form:

```python
from cantus import Skill

class SearchBook(Skill):
    """Search the library catalog."""
    name = "search_book"
    def run(self, title: str, lang: str = "zh-TW") -> str: ...
```

## Analyzer — parse text into a typed value

```python
from cantus import analyzer
from pydantic import BaseModel

class Book(BaseModel):
    title: str
    isbn: str

@analyzer
def parse_book_list(text: str) -> list[Book]:
    """Parse search_book output into Book models."""
    ...
```

## Validator — return Result(ok, value, feedback)

```python
from cantus import validator, Result

@validator
def ensure_isbn_valid(book: Book) -> Result:
    """Verify ISBN-13 checksum."""
    if checksum_ok(book.isbn):
        return Result.success(book)
    return Result.failure(f"ISBN checksum mismatch on {book.isbn}")
```

The agent loop wraps `Result(ok=False, ...)` as a `ValidationErrorObservation` and retries the previous action up to `max_retries` (default 3).

## Workflow — orchestrate skills, analyzers, validators

```python
from cantus import workflow

@workflow
def recommend_books(topic: str, n: int = 3) -> list[Book]:
    """Search → parse → validate."""
    raw = search_book(topic=topic, n=n)
    parsed = parse_book_list(raw)
    return [b for b in parsed if ensure_isbn_valid(b).ok]
```

A workflow is a regular Python function (or class with `run`). Calls to other registered protocols inside the body go through the registry and appear in the `EventStream` automatically.

## Memory — class-only, three reference implementations

```python
from cantus import Memory
from cantus.protocols.memory import Turn

class TopicMemory(Memory):
    """Remember topics the student has asked about."""

    def __init__(self):
        self.topics: list[str] = []

    def remember(self, turn: Turn) -> None:
        self.topics.append(turn.user)

    def recall(self, query: str) -> list[Turn]:
        return [Turn(user=t, assistant="") for t in self.topics if query in t]
```

Built-in implementations:

- `ShortTermMemory(n=10)` — most recent N turns (`collections.deque`)
- `BM25Memory(top_k=5)` — keyword retrieval (requires `rank-bm25`)
- `EmbeddingMemory(top_k=5)` — semantic retrieval (requires `sentence-transformers`)

## Tool-call grammar (for reference)

The agent constrains the LLM output to:

```json
{
  "thought": "<free-form reasoning text>",
  "action": {"skill_name": "<one-of-registered>", "args": {...}}
}
```

or, to terminate:

```json
{
  "thought": "<free-form>",
  "action": {"final_answer": "<string>"}
}
```

`thought` is **never** constrained by grammar — only `skill_name` (whitelist of registered skills) and `args` (Pydantic schema of the named skill) are.

## Common patterns

```python
# Three-protocol pipeline
@workflow
def pipeline(query: str):
    raw = search_book(query)
    parsed = parse_book_list(raw)
    return [b for b in parsed if ensure_isbn_valid(b).ok]

# @debug stacking — opt-in trace
from cantus import debug
@debug
@skill
def traced_search(title: str) -> str: ...
# stdout will include: [debug] traced_search args=... result=...

# Loading the model
from cantus import mount_drive_and_load, Agent
model = mount_drive_and_load(variant="E4B")  # or "E2B"
agent = Agent(model=model)
state = agent.run("Recommend three sci-fi books", max_iterations=8)
```

## Style rules for generated code

- Always use type annotations on every parameter and return.
- Always include a Google-style docstring with description and `Args:` block.
- Decorator goes on the line *immediately above* the function definition.
- Stack `@debug` *above* `@skill` (or other protocol decorator), never below.
- For validators, always return `Result.success(...)` or `Result.failure(...)`.
- Memory subclasses always implement both `recall(query)` and `remember(turn)`.
